output.task_description: Canine (Beagle) female, 6 years old, presenting with seizures and disorientation; prior idiopathic epilepsy on phenobarbital; normal CBC/biochem/MRI, generalized epileptiform EEG.

solution_steps.title: Compile existing diagnostics
actions: Summarize CBC, biochemistry, MRI, and EEG results.
Document current medication (phenobarbital) and dosage history.

solution_steps.title: Identify gaps in data
actions: Check for therapeutic drug monitoring of phenobarbital.
Evaluate sleep quality and environmental stressors.
Consider behavioral assessment for seizure triggers.

solution_steps.title: Order additional tests
actions: Phenobarbital serum concentration with target 20–40 µg/mL.
Optional: repeat EEG after dose adjustment if seizures persist.

solution_steps.title: Adjust therapy based on results
actions: If sub‑therapeutic, titrate phenobarbital upward within safe range.
If toxic or electrolytes abnormal, reduce dose or switch to levetiracetam/ketogenic diet.
Monitor CBC/biochem every 3–4 weeks during adjustments.

solution_steps.title: Client education and monitoring plan
actions: Explain importance of consistent dosing schedule.
Teach owners to recognize signs of phenobarbital toxicity (vomiting, ataxia).
Discuss sleep hygiene and reducing stressors.
Schedule follow‑up in 2 months or sooner if seizures recur.

quality_self_assessment.justification: All recommendations are evidence‑based, directly address identified gaps, and align with current veterinary guidelines for idiopathic epilepsy management.
